The headline is that the launch is on track.
Here is a brief recap of the main highlights.
The key takeaway is a positive outcome.
In short, the launch is on track.
First, the system is live. Then, the team is trained.
Overall, adoption is high.
The key takeaway is that we are on schedule.
In short, the launch is on track. First, the system is live; overall, adoption is high. The key takeaway: we are on schedule.
Daniela, the board only has five minutes. How will you open?
With the headline: in short, the launch is on track. Then a brief recap of the main points.
Good. What are the highlights?
And the one risk? Keep it soft.
It seems there is a minor delay, but we have a workaround. The outcome is still positive.
Perfect. How do you close?
The key takeaway is that we are on schedule. The next step is the final review on Friday. That is the gist.
Overall, the main highlight is high adoption.
The next step is the final review on Friday.

Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. This is your final lesson, and today we bring everything together into one skill: the executive summary. Busy leaders have very little time, so they want the main point first. The rule is simple. Headline first, details later. Start with the outcome in one sentence. To sum up, the launch is on track with a minor delay. Then give a short recap of the key points. Use signposting words to guide the listener. First, then, finally, and overall. Keep it concise. A good summary is brief and clear, never long. Add the takeaway at the end, the one thing you want people to remember. The key takeaway is that we are still on schedule. And remember everything you learned this year. Connect cause and result with because and so. Soften the risks with it seems and might. Then close with a clear next step. So, headline, recap, takeaway, next step. That is the executive summary. I am so proud of your progress, Daniela. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus. I am leaving you a quick voicemail before your board presentation tomorrow. You will give the executive summary of the whole project, so let me give you the structure. Open with the headline. In short, the project is a success, with one small caveat. Then give a brief overview of the highlights. The new system is live, the team is trained, and adoption is high. Keep each point concise. After the highlights, recap the one risk. It seems there is a minor delay on the reporting module, but we have a workaround. Because the testing took longer, the final report may slip by a week. Then close with the takeaway and the next step. The key takeaway is that the launch is on track. The next step is the final review on Friday. Lead with the outcome, keep it short, and end with one clear takeaway. You have come so far this year, Daniela. Go and own that boardroom. Call me after. Talk soon.
